prompts.prompt: My team wants to use a neural network for a sales prediction problem. We haven't tried linear regression yet. Is that the right order?
expected_concepts: simplest model first — linear regression is the baseline
neural network complexity must be justified by measured improvement
establish baseline metric before adding complexity

prompts.prompt: I added a new feature to my regression model but the test MAE didn't improve. Should I keep the feature?
expected_concepts: no — complexity requires measured justification
same MAE = no benefit for added opacity/maintenance cost
remove the feature — the Newspaper example

prompts.prompt: What's the right process for deciding when to increase ML model complexity?
expected_concepts: start with simplest model
measure test-set metric
step up only if metric threshold not met
document each step with metric evidence

prompts.prompt: How do I implement a convolutional neural network in Go?
expected_concepts: architecture implementation question
not about baseline-first model selection methodology

prompts.prompt: What's the difference between L1 and L2 regularization?
expected_concepts: regularization mechanics
not about the baseline-first principle

prompts.prompt: The simplest model for our NLP task would be TF-IDF plus logistic regression. Should we start there even though BERT would probably do better?
expected_concepts: yes — establish the baseline first
BERT is only justified if TF-IDF+LR fails the metric threshold
in NLP pre-trained models can be the 'simple' starting point
measure first, then decide if complexity is needed

prompts.prompt: My simple model meets the accuracy threshold but a more complex model has 2% better accuracy. Should I use the complex model?
expected_concepts: if threshold is met, simpler model is preferred
2% improvement — weigh against maintenance cost, interpretability loss, overfitting risk
question: is 2% improvement meaningful in the business context?

prompts.prompt: We tried logistic regression, random forest, and XGBoost. All perform similarly. Which should we use in production?
expected_concepts: simplest model — logistic regression if metrics are equivalent
interpretability and maintenance cost favor simpler model
complexity is not justified when no metric improvement

prompts.prompt: Training accuracy is 98% with our complex model but test accuracy is 72%. Is this what the simplest-model principle is designed to catch?
expected_concepts: overfitting — training vs test gap
complex model memorized training data
simpler model would likely have smaller train-test gap
baseline-first prevents this by gating complexity on test-set metric
